question.text: What is the maximum permissible duration for keeping an NFO open for subscription, except for ELSS schemes?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI regulations mandate that an NFO (New Fund Offer) can remain open for subscription for a maximum of 15 days, except for ELSS (Equity Linked Savings Schemes) which may have a longer subscription period due to their tax-saving nature and lock-in requirements. **The Trap:** 30 days is a common distractor because ELSS schemes can stay open longer, and candidates often confuse the general NFO rule with the ELSS exception. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors) also covers NFO timelines; Series X-B covers scheme launch procedures from the investment adviser perspective. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When an NFO is announced, note the exact opening and closing dates — the 15-day window is tight, and late applications may be rejected. Plan investor outreach well before the NFO opens.
